en_body:
spring follows the two small birds home to perch upon the wire clotheslines behind the house,
you and I go out into the grassy yard seeking a place for the coming season's vegetable garden,
--This corner, listen dear, beside the kitchen, near the water-pump, easier to water the garden in the afternoon!

you change into a short-sleeved shirt, take the hoe to break and dig...
I sow mustard seeds into the earth you have just turned,
I sow melon seeds beneath the bamboo trellis you have just made,
I sow corn seeds in the bed you have just raised,
I sow bean seeds in the little holes you have just dug.

the sun has gone down, only gentle afternoon light and quiet wind remain,
wiping the drops of sweat, you lean against the oak, standing to watch my small figure,
pretty short hair, sitting in the middle of the garden...
I lift my head laughing, wave my hand and tell you,
--Such a cool wind, dear...this afternoon the sky is truly beautiful!
 My dear, hoe me a little piece of earth over there
 So I may plant a chrysanthemum bush, and sow a bed of lilies beside this corner!

I wear a white áo bà-ba, round shoulders like the petals of a night-blooming cereus,
sitting slightly inclined, intent, I speak and laugh like sun-stars falling.
hearing me speak is enough to make you joyful too
when you finish hoeing the earth, I wait to hand you a glass of iced lemonade,
--Look, dear, how very beautiful our garden is!

hoeing the earth wearies both arms,
hearing me talk and laugh, you too rejoice, because I look like a ray of sunlight.

coming home from work in the afternoon, you sit reading the paper,
I make the rice, cooking inside our home...
after the meal, we go into the garden to water the earth,
--My dear, look, there is a sprout rising here!

afternoon after afternoon, eagerly awaiting
one more young branch, one more blade of leaf...
Happiness green-green inside clear eyes,
upon your lips and mine laughter falls as sun-stars flowering into the spring garden.

this afternoon is Friday afternoon, coming home from work wearier than usual,
you sit in the rattan chair, I pour you a glass of clear water,
I set the meal, with a bowl of chrysanthemum-green soup from the garden we husband and wife planted,
with a plate of beans stir-fried with beef, the beans still crisp, sweet and fresh, cooling all through our Love.
I look at you and smile:
--Eat, dear. Vegetables and beans from our own garden, so delicious, aren't they?

sitting upon the veranda step, we watch mustard flowers bloom fresh-yellow in their beds,
there are white butterflies flying aimlessly, lingering;
the cantaloupe trellis, skins deepening golden,
the ears of corn, their silk already dry, nearly ready to gather:
my little flower garden, blossoms showing every color,
--The Chrysanthemums will bloom soon, dear! Watering the vegetables this morning, I had just noticed.
 Autumn is almost here...so quickly, isn't it, dear?

sunlight slowly pales, the sky softly dims, mosquitoes drift and fly,
we two return inside, I latch the screen door...
--Dear! it seems all the cicadas outside in the garden have died.

the wind blows vi-vu, red maple leaves fall and cover the garden,
the bitter-melon, squash, and gourd trellises shrivel upon dry vines,
mustard flowers wither into seeds,
I have gathered them in, saving them for seed,
next spring...

We shall make our garden again, you and I..!!


(A small and simple gift: Blessings of a Hundred Years of Happiness
 to Hoàng(LuiKang) and Wife)
note: The áo bà-ba is a traditional southern Vietnamese tunic, and “a Hundred Years of Happiness” is a conventional blessing for married life.
